original_abstract:
While not everyone leaves behind a written autobiography, the Bible accords a unique prominence to the life of Abraham, preserving his journey as a spiritual legacy for contemporary believers. Within this narrative, Abraham’s intercession for Sodom and Gomorrah (Genesis 18:16-33) stands as a particularly compelling episode. Faced with God’s resolve to judge the two cities, Abraham entered into a bold negotiation, pleading for mercy in the hope of averting total destruction. Ultimately, although the cities were not spared, the rescue of Lot and his daughters underscores the profound impact of Abraham's intervention. 

This research employs narrative interpretation and a comprehensive literature review to frame Abraham's intercessory act through the specific lens of Pentecostal spirituality. The study aims to clarify the spiritual contours of Abraham’s life and extract deeper theological meaning from his dialogue with the Divine. The findings suggest that from a Pentecostal perspective, Abraham’s intercession is more than a historical record; it is a model of persistent faith and courageous compassion. It serves as a biblical archetype for the mission of the modern church, representing a proactive, Spirit-empowered engagement with the world that seeks justice and mercy simultaneously.
humanized_abstract:
Few of us write autobiographies about our lives, but the Bible makes Abraham one of the central characters in the story of humanity. Perhaps one of the most fascinating chapters in all the Bible is Genesis 18:16-33, in which Abraham intercedes for Sodom and Gomorrah. When God declared His intention to destroy the cities, Abraham set out to persuade God not to do what God had promised to do. Abraham bargained with God, asking if God would spare the cities if there were only ten righteous people in each. The fact that God finally spared Lot and his two daughters shows how crucial Abraham’s intercession was.

This paper explores Abraham's intercession as seen through a Pentecostal lens using narrative interpretation and a critical review of scripture. The main objective of this paper is to unveil the spiritual landscape of Abraham's life and thus retrieve more theological contents from the dialogue between Abraham and God. Using a Pentecostal lens the intercession of Abraham as recorded in the Bible turns out to be more than just a historical record. Abraham's intercession provides a model of what it takes for one to be faithful and to act in incredible compassion towards people without regard to cost. Hence it is an exemplary biblical model of the kind of role the church today is to play in the world in a mission of evangelism. This will entail active engagement with society that is Spirit-empowered, justice-orientated and at the same time charitable.
